title: LootLedger
description: LootLedger tracks every container interaction in your world. See who opened a chest, what items were added or removed, and when it happened, in singleplayer and multiplayer. Simply open any container and click the book icon to view its full history.
body:
# LootLedger
is a Fabric mod that tracks every interaction with containers in your Minecraft world. Whether you're playing solo or running a multiplayer server, LootLedger gives you full transparency over who accessed your chests, barrels, shulker boxes, and more — and exactly what they did.
- All languages are supported.

![Detail_list](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/I73SkmZm/images/451d17407c1a7247b05d3f70b7b467d32270112d.png)

## How it works
Simply open any container and click the book icon next to it. A clean overlay will appear showing the full history of that container — who accessed it, what items were added or removed, how many, and at what time. The history is stored persistently per world, meaning it survives server restarts and is always up to date.

## Features
- 📦 Tracks all vanilla containers — chests, double chests, barrels, shulker boxes, hoppers,
dispensers, droppers and more
- 🧩 Modded items are automatically recognized and displayed with their correct names and icons — no extra setup required

- 🔧 Automatically works with modded containers — no configuration needed

- 🖥️ Clean and intuitive overlay UI with item icons, player avatars, timestamps and hover tooltips

- 🔍 Scroll through the full history of any container
💾 Persistent storage — all data is saved and survives world restarts

- 🌍 Works in both singleplayer and multiplayer

- ⚡ Lightweight and performance friendly — only tracks when containers are opened

- ⚙️ Configurable entry limit — choose between 32, 64, 128 (default), 256, or Unlimited entries per container via the in-game settings panel

- 🔒 Server-friendly permissions — on multiplayer servers, only operators can change the entry limit. The setting applies to all players and is clearly visible to everyone.

![Icon](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/I73SkmZm/images/bbb4012e7f5f9882037df17f95b3e6115ff6626e.png)

**Use cases**
Find out who took items from your shared storage on a multiplayer server
Keep track of your own container interactions in singleplayer
Identify griefing or theft on your server without needing admin commands

**Requirements**
Minecraft 26.1
Fabric Loader 0.18.4 or higher
Fabric API

**Known Issues**
All known bugs have been fixed in the latest update. Please report any further bugs via GitHub issues if you encounter them.
